//: Project-level Claude Code settings. Comments use the '//' key convention (settings.json tolerates string-valued '//' keys).

permissions.//: Pre-approved commands so Claude doesn't ask each time. Narrow on purpose — broaden via /fewer-permission-prompts after a few sessions of friction.

//hooks: Hooks run shell commands the harness enforces — Claude can't forget them. These are Windows-PowerShell friendly and gracefully no-op if the tool isn't installed yet (e.g. before `uv sync`).

hooks.//PostToolUse-ruff: Lint Python files after every Edit/Write. Skips non-.py files and no-ops if ruff isn't on PATH yet.

hooks.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "$f = $env:CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_file_path; if ($f -match '\.py$' -and (Get-Command ruff -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { ruff check $f 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 20 }"

hooks.//example-test-on-edit: Run pytest for the touched module when a test file is edited. Uncomment when tests/ exists.

hooks.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "$f = $env:CLAUDE_TOOL_INPUT_file_path; if ($f -match 'test_.*\.py$|.*_test\.py$' -and (Get-Command pytest -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)) { pytest -q $f 2>&1 | Select-Object -Last 30 }"

hooks.//example-UserPromptSubmit-git: Inject git status into every prompt. Useful once this is a git repo.

hooks.command: powershell -NoProfile -Command "if (Test-Path .git) { Write-Output '<git>'; git status --short; Write-Output '---'; git log --oneline -5; Write-Output '</git>' }"

hooks.//example-Stop-notify: Toast notification when Claude finishes a turn.

//env: Project-specific env vars Claude should see (e.g. data paths, seeds). Empty for now.
